what_it_does: It is a generated surface specification for view C5, declaring its identity, platform, represented concepts, status, home journey, UI addresses, and build-time constraints for components, lint, and tokens.
observations: Generated surface-spec labeled id C5 in area C with title 'Replay / decision trajectory audit'.
Status is 'quality-passed' while journeys and cross_journeys are empty.
Lists six ui:// addresses across activity, inbox, and inspector namespaces.
build_to names components activity, inbox, inspector and a design lint command plus a tokens source.
standouts: journeys and cross_journeys are empty even though the file describes them as the address-refs that carry the web.
Status is 'quality-passed' with no supporting evidence in the file.
build_to mixes components, a lint shell command, and a token pipeline in one object.
conventions: Follows a ledger surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses ui:// namespace addresses as canonical references.
Declares design-system.css as the only look-source derived from tokens.json.
Includes a self-describing _what field explaining provenance and constraints.
concerns: Empty journeys/cross_journeys contradict the description that they carry the web.
Quality-passed status is asserted without file-local justification.
build_to.design_lint is a free-form shell command rather than a structured reference.
No symbols are listed, limiting code-level traceability.
notes: A desktop-only replay/audit surface spec that records addresses and build targets but leaves its journey graph blank.
questions: Why are journeys and cross_journeys empty if they are meant to carry the web?
What does B3 refer to in the _what description?
Does 'quality-passed' indicate human review, automated check, or a milestone label?
How do the six addresses map to the three build_to components?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to specify the C5 replay/decision-trajectory audit surface; the file does that structurally, but the empty journey arrays make the routing/trajectory part incomplete.
apparent_themes: Design-system fidelity through a single token source
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to pin down a replay/audit view, constrain its build to specific components and lint/token pipelines, and prevent fabricated addresses by anchoring everything to a fixed address list.
novelty: The explicit anti-invention rule in _what and the inclusion of a lint command and token pipeline inside build_to are unusual for a surface spec.
contribution: It contributes a structured record of the C5 surface's identity, intended UI addresses, target components, and build constraints that a fused system can keep as a reference node.
summary_for_embedding: C5.json is a generated desktop surface specification for a replay and decision-trajectory audit view, listing UI addresses, build components, lint and token constraints, and an empty journey graph.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to define the C5 audit/replay surface and prescribe how it should be built and styled without inventing addresses.
